patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v7 00/10] dax/kmem: atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs

version_history.subject: [PATCH v6 00/10] dax/kmem: atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs
[PATCH v7 00/10] dax/kmem: atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 01/10] mm/memory: add memory_block_aligned_range() helper
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new helper function called memory_block_aligned_range() to the Linux kernel's mm/memory module. The function takes an input range and returns a new range that is aligned to memory block boundaries, which is necessary for memory hotplug operations. The function also guards against end underflow by returning an empty range if the input range falls below the first memory block boundary. The patch updates the dax/kmem driver to use this new helper function.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 02/10] mm/memory_hotplug: add mhp_online_type_to_str() and export string helpers
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds two string helper functions to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug module: mhp_online_type_to_str() and mhp_online_type_from_str(). The first function converts an online type integer into a human-readable string, while the second does the opposite. Both functions are exported so that drivers can use them in their sysfs interfaces.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 03/10] mm/memory_hotplug: pass online_type to online_memory_block() via arg
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the `online_memory_block` function to accept an explicit online type through its argument, rather than using a default value. This change prepares for future scenarios where callers can specify their own online types. The patch also updates the `add_memory_resource` function to pass the default online type as a local variable. No functional changes are made, and the patch is reviewed by several kernel developers.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 04/10] mm/memory_hotplug: export mhp_get_default_online_type
series_items.patch_summary: This patch exports the `mhp_get_default_online_type` function from the Linux kernel's memory hotplug module to allow drivers that use DAX (Direct Access) to build and function even when hotplug is disabled. The function returns a default online type, which in this case is non-destructive (`MMOP_OFFLINE`). This change enables co-mingling of hotplug and devdax use cases within the same driver code without breaking other non-hotplug use cases.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 05/10] mm/memory_hotplug: add __add_memory_driver_managed() with online_type arg
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new interface to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug functionality, allowing drivers to select their preferred online type (ZONE_NORMAL or ZONE_MOVABLE) when adding memory blocks. The CXL driver is the primary motivator for this change, as it needs to plumb a preferred policy through the dax kmem driver. The patch refactors existing APIs and adds proper kernel documentation while introducing no functional changes for existing users. The new interface is restricted to in-tree modules using EXPORT_SYMBOL_FOR_MODULES().

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 06/10] mm/memory_hotplug: add offline_and_remove_memory_ranges()
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called offline_and_remove_memory_ranges() to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug module. The function takes an array of memory ranges and processes them as one operation under a single lock, ensuring atomicity and preventing race conditions between system daemons trying to manage the same resource. It first offlines every block in all ranges, then removes the ranges only if all are offline. If any offline step fails, the whole operation is reverted, leaving the device in a consistent state. This patch also updates the existing offline_and_remove_memory() function to use the new helper and move its logic into one place.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 07/10] dax/kmem: resolve default online type at probe time
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the kmem driver to explicitly set the default online type at probe time, rather than inheriting it from the system default. This change is made by calling a new function `__add_memory_driver_managed` that takes an additional argument for the online type. The behavior of the code remains unchanged, but this modification sets the stage for future changes related to memory management and NUMA policies.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 08/10] dax/kmem: extract hotplug/hotremove helper functions
series_items.patch_summary: This patch refactors the kmem probe and remove functions by extracting initialization, cleanup, hotplug, and hot-remove logic into separate helper functions. The new helpers enable future extensions to support more granular control over memory hotplug operations. This is a pure refactoring with no functional change, allowing for re-use of hotunplug code without destroying underlying resources.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 09/10] dax/kmem: add sysfs interface for atomic whole-device hotplug
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysfs interface to enable atomic whole-device hotplug operations for DAX kmem devices. The new 'state' attribute allows devices to be transitioned between states such as unplugged, online, and offline_kernel or offline_movable. This addresses a race condition where another entity could operate on memory blocks while the device is being removed, causing hot-unplug failures or deadlocks. The patch also introduces an 'unplugged' state for DAX devices to indicate that memory blocks are not present. Valid transitions between states are defined, and existing tools are preserved by creating memory blocks at probe time.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v7 10/10] selftests/dax: add dax/kmem hotplug sysfs regression test
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a selftest to verify the functionality of dax/kmem hotplug sysfs attributes. The test transitions a kmem-backed dax device between 'unplugged', 'online' and 'online_movable' states, ensuring that memory is correctly added or removed from the system. It also checks for idempotence, invalid input rejection, and correct behavior when toggling blocks out-of-band through the per-block memory interface. A specific regression test case is included to ensure re-onlineing properly reserves resources after an unplug operation.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series introduces atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs for the dax/kmem driver, allowing for a single operation to offline and remove an entire multi-block DAX kmem device without creating a race condition between userland controllers. The series includes refactoring of existing code, addition of new helper functions, and implementation of the sysfs interface for atomic whole-device hotplug.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Addressed all issues raised by Sashiko reviews, confirming that the series is clean as-is.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] mm/memory-tiers: cache top tier nodes
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the memory-tiers code by caching top-tier nodes on memory node hotplug events, reducing redundant tier distance checks in various hot paths.

review_comments.author: Aneesh Kumar K.V
review_comments.summary: Raised concern that caching top-tier nodes could lead to a larger window of incorrect results from node_is_toptier() calls, and questioned the performance impact.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes

version_history.subject: [PATCH] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes
[PATCH v2] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes
[PATCH v3] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix PAGEMAP_SCAN written state for PMD holes

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel where PAGEMAP_SCAN incorrectly reports an unpopulated PMD hole as not written, leading to data corruption after live migration or checkpoint/restore operations. The fix adds a check for PMD holes and reports them as written, matching the behavior of pte_none handling. The patch also excludes hugetlb entries, which are handled differently by the kernel. A selftest is added to cover the anon-THP PMD-hole case.

review_comments.author: Zenghui Yu
review_comments.summary:
The reviewer pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where a huge page is split into two smaller pages, and suggested adding a check to handle this scenario.

The reviewer reported a compilation error on mm-new due to an undeclared identifier 'MADV_COLLAPSE' in the pagemap_ioctl.c file, suggesting that the fix from fd5295afae91 should be applied.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 7/9/26 8:16 PM, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

I hit the following compilation error on mm-new:

[root@localhost mm]# make
  CC       pagemap_ioctl
pagemap_ioctl.c: In function 'unpopulated_thp_hole_test':
pagemap_ioctl.c:1130:31: error: 'MADV_COLLAPSE' undeclared (first use in this function); did you mean 'MADV_COLD'?
 1130 |  if (madvise(mem, hpage_size, MADV_COLLAPSE) ||
      |                               ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                               MADV_COLD
pagemap_ioctl.c:1130:31: note: each undeclared identifier is reported only once for each function it appears in
make: *** [../lib.mk:225: /root/code/mm/tools/testing/selftests/mm/pagemap_ioctl] Error 1

Could you consider addressing it like fd5295afae91 ("selftests/mm:
hmm-tests: include linux/mman.h to access MADV_COLLAPSE")?

Thanks,
Zenghui
